summary: A ragged audience tape that still dazzles thanks to a smoking Eleven → St. Stephen run and Jerry’s soaring lead work.
review: Listeners rave about the historic June‑68 performance – especially the rare feedback→Eleven→St. Stephen sandwich, the fiery Alligator transition and Jerry Garcia’s vocal/guitar intensity.  The recording is low‑fidelity audience tape with clipping and muffled vocals, but fans say it rewards careful listening on good headphones.

ai_rating.rationale: The show is repeatedly described as one of the best of ’68 with standout jams, while the recording quality is consistently called ragged or terrible; weighting 70 % performance and 30 % audio yields a solid four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Low‑fidelity audience tape – heavy clipping, barely audible vocals, overall muddy mix; however headphones can mitigate noise and reveal the performance’s energy.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual feedback sandwich opens and closes the show, leading into a rare feedback→Eleven→St. Stephen sequence; odd Alligator→Lovelight transition adds intrigue despite Lovelight’s middling execution.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead guitar and vocal delivery are highlighted as exceptional, carrying the whole performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Occasional vocal harmonies from Phil Fill noted; no specific bass commentary.
